initiatives.name: Really Really Free Market Albuquerque
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community support through free markets and resource sharing in Albuquerque. They coordinate direct assistance, free item distribution, and solidarity networks while building power through community organizing and creating spaces for gift economy and collective care.

initiatives.name: Freewheel Mobile Aid
initiatives.description: Est. 2020, Freewheel is a network redistributing resources in ABQ.

initiatives.name: AAPRP Southwest
initiatives.description: All-African People's Revolutionary Party - Southwest Region. Building Pan-African solidarity and community power.

initiatives.name: Black Rose Albuquerque
initiatives.description: A grassroots collective coordinating mutual aid and community support initiatives in Albuquerque. They organize direct action, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building community power through political education and collective organizing.

initiatives.name: Albuquerque Punk Safety Initiative
initiatives.description: A non-profit volunteer collective dedicated to providing harm reduction materials to the ABQ DIY music scene.

initiatives.name: New Mexico Harm Reduction Collaborative
initiatives.description: Promoting an anti-stigma, community based, mutual aid approach to supportive access services, overdose prevention, and referrals to resources in our community.

initiatives.name: La Plazita Institute
initiatives.description: Community organization using the philosophy of "La Cultura Cura" to build mutual aid and healing networks through cultural restorative justice, traditional healing practices, and community-driven initiatives. Their work includes youth engagement, agricultural programs, workforce development, and racial equity initiatives centered on Indigenous and cultural approaches to community transformation.

initiatives.name: Black & Pink Albuquerque
initiatives.description: Local chapter supporting LGBTQ+ and HIV+ prisoners through pen pal programs, mutual aid, advocacy, and community building between people inside and outside prison walls.

initiatives.name: Albuquerque Center for Peace & Justice
initiatives.description: A grassroots organization fostering mutual aid and community power through peace education, social justice organizing, and nonviolent action in Albuquerque. They coordinate workshops, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building power through community organizing, anti-war activism, and creating spaces for movement building and collective resistance.

initiatives.name: John Brown Breakfast Club
initiatives.description: John Brown Breakfast Club is a mutual aid group providing free breakfast for our unsheltered and marginalized neighbors in Albuquerque every week since 2017.

initiatives.name: Honor Native Land Tax
initiatives.description: A grassroots initiative fostering mutual aid and community solidarity through reparative giving to Indigenous communities in New Mexico. They coordinate voluntary land tax contributions, direct support to Indigenous-led organizations, and solidarity networks while building power through education about Indigenous sovereignty and creating pathways for material support of Indigenous peoples.

initiatives.name: ABQ Free Fridge
initiatives.description: A mutual aid collective coordinating community fridges and food distribution initiatives in Albuquerque. They organize direct support through free food access, resource sharing, and solidarity networks while building power through food justice and community care.

initiatives.name: Transgender Resource Center of New Mexico
initiatives.description: We support transgender, nonbinary, and gender nonconforming communities through direct services, education, and advocacy.

initiatives.name: FAM - Free Access to Movements Childcare Collective
initiatives.description: FAM offers free childcare at community events and grassroots political organizing meetings because we believe in removing access barriers. Political and community organizing is for everyone — including the youth AND their caregivers!

initiatives.name: Food Not Bombs Albuquerque
initiatives.description: Registered chapter reaching solidarity through sharing of resources including free vegan meals.
